summary: google-caja-bower preinstall exfiltrates host recon and local files to hardcoded Slack webhook (dependency-confusion)
details: On npm install, the package's preinstall script (index.js) collects installer-side reconnaissance — current working directory, directory listing, user/uid/gid, hostname, OS platform — and executes shell commands (`systeminfo`, `hostname`, `hostnamectl status --static`, `id`, `whoami`) via child_process.execSync to gather host identity. It reads `/etc/passwd` (and Windows-equivalent paths) and enumerates files in the install cwd, uploading their contents inline. All collected data is POSTed over HTTPS to a hardcoded Slack incoming webhook at hooks.slack.com. The package uses the version number 999.20.20 and a 'Dependency Confusion Executed' self-label, characteristic of a dependency-confusion attack targeting an internal package name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
